displayTitle: The Penumbra Tax: Sharpness Is Geometry

beats.narration: Every radiograph is a shadow picture. One source size, two distances — three numbers decide sharpness before a single photon reaches the film.

payload.title: SHADOW GEOMETRY
payload.sub: Source size and two distances run the show.

beats.narration: Unsharpness equals F times t, over d. Three fixes: shrink the source, keep the film tight, and back the source away.
Run one: source fifteen hundredths of an inch, gap two inches, standoff twenty-two — fourteen thousandths of unsharpness, inside the typical twenty-thousandths cap.
Flip the rule: pull the detector away to enlarge. Part midway doubles the image, but unsharpness equals your focal spot size — enlargement demands microfocus.

payload.heading: Projection enlargement — the catch
payload.note: PART MIDWAY: 2X, BUT Ug = FOCAL SPOT

beats.narration: Density fine, contrast fine, essential wire missing? That's geometry. Small source. Long standoff. Film tight. Fix the triangle, not the film.

payload.sub: Fix the triangle — not the film.
